title: Make deployment invariant guard linked-worktree-path safe
goal: Harden Leitstand's deprecated deployment-entry-point guard so validation ignores Git administrative metadata of linked worktrees and cannot fail solely because a linked-worktree path contains the deprecated token `leitstand-deploy`, while continuing to reject real deprecated references in repository-controlled content.

approval.note: Follow-up discovered during OPERATOR-MACHINE-READABILITY-V1-T024 live deployment on 2026-07-24. Preserve fail-closed rejection of real deprecated deployment references; narrow only the linked-worktree .git metadata false-positive.

acceptance.assertion: The runbook invariant guard does not inspect linked-worktree Git administrative metadata such as a root `.git` file when searching repository-controlled content for deprecated deployment entry points.
A real `leitstand-deploy` reference in tracked repository-controlled content still fails the guard.
A regression test proves validation succeeds from a linked worktree whose filesystem path itself contains `leitstand-deploy` when repository content is otherwise clean.
The fix changes only validation scope and creates no deployment, runtime, merge or mutation authority.

metadata.observation: An exact merged T024 deployment from linked worktree path leitstand-deploy-t024-final-v1 failed before cutover because the guard grep inspected the linked-worktree root .git file and matched the path token itself. The same exact commit deployed successfully from neutral path ls-t024-final-v1.
